disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No post-auricular swelling/redness or the ear being pushed forward (mastoiditis); no neck stiffness, drowsiness, non-blanching rash (meningitis)
trigger_question_examples: Is there any red, tender swelling on the bone behind the ear, or is the ear pushed forward?
Is he drowsy/hard to rouse, with a stiff neck or a rash?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No swelling behind the ear, he's alert, no stiff neck and no rash.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Systemically well enough — alert/consolable, still drinking, adequate wet nappies, no persistent vomiting; low-grade (not very high) fever
trigger_question_examples: Is he still drinking and having wet nappies, and is he rousable and consolable?
Any repeated vomiting or a very high fever?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's drinking, having normal wet nappies, alert and consolable in my arms, no vomiting.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Unilateral ear pain after a viral URI, no ear discharge; not pain on pinna traction — middle-ear (AOM) rather than canal (otitis externa)
trigger_question_examples: Did it follow a cold, and is there any discharge from the ear?
Does pulling on the outer ear itself hurt him a lot?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came after a cold, no discharge, and pulling the outer ear isn't the main thing that hurts.
